content.text: HTML provides more than 20 distinct input types. Choosing the right type does three things at once: it adapts the browser UI to the expected data (numeric keyboard for numbers, date picker for dates), it enables built-in client-side validation, and it communicates intent to assistive technologies. This topic covers every input type, their key attributes, browser validation behaviour, and how to bind them in Angular reactive forms.

attrs.alt: HTML input types reference map — text, numeric, date/time, choice, and special inputs with Angular binding patterns
attrs.title: HTML Input Types Reference Map

content.text: All text-based inputs share a core set of attributes. The difference lies in built-in validation and the keyboard/UI the browser surfaces on mobile.
required, placeholder, maxlength, minlength, pattern, autocomplete, readonly, disabled, spellcheck, inputmode (overrides keyboard hint)

<!-- Pattern: 5-digit US ZIP code -->
<label for="zip">ZIP code</label>
<input id="zip" type="text" name="zip"
       pattern="\d{5}" maxlength="5"
       inputmode="numeric"
       placeholder="12345" required />

<!-- autocomplete: browser fills saved values -->
<input type="email" name="email" autocomplete="email" />
<input type="password" name="pw"  autocomplete="current-password" />
overrides the keyboard hint without changing validation semantics. inputmode="numeric" shows a numeric keypad on a type="text" field when you do not want the spinner arrows of type="number".
values follow the WHATWG Autofill specification (username, new-password, given-name, cc-number, etc.) and are used by both browsers and password managers.
takes a JavaScript regular expression (without / delimiters). The whole value must match — anchors ^ and $ are implicit.

type="number" renders a spinner and validates that the value is a number within the allowed range. type="range" renders a slider — the value is always within bounds, but the user cannot see the current value without extra markup.

<!-- Spinner with step constraints -->
<label for="qty">Quantity (multiples of 5)</label>
<input id="qty" type="number" name="qty"
       min="0" max="500" step="5" value="10" required />

<!-- Slider with live value display -->
<label for="vol">Volume: <output id="volOut">50</output>%</label>
<input id="vol" type="range" name="volume"
       min="0" max="100" step="1" value="50"
       oninput="volOut.value = this.value" />
work on both types. For number, step also determines which values pass stepMismatch validation.
removes the step constraint — any numeric value is valid.
The value attribute is always a
in the DOM. Parse with Number() or parseInt() before arithmetic.
Avoid type="number" for things like credit-card numbers, phone numbers, or ZIP codes — use type="text" with inputmode="numeric" and pattern instead, since leading zeros and hyphens are common.
All date/time types submit their value in ISO 8601 format regardless of the user's locale. Browser UIs vary significantly — always test on your target platforms.
Native calendar picker; min/max use same format
No timezone — stores local time as-is

<!-- Date range: must be between two dates -->
<input type="date" name="arrival"
       min="2025-07-01" max="2025-07-31" required />

<!-- Time: quarter-hour slots (step in seconds) -->
<input type="time" name="slot" step="900" />

<!-- datetime-local: meeting scheduler -->
<input type="datetime-local" name="meeting"
       min="2025-07-01T09:00" max="2025-07-31T17:00" />
datetime-local stores no timezone. If your app is timezone-sensitive, store UTC and convert on the server. Consider a third-party date picker for production forms that need reliable cross-browser UX.
A checkbox submits its value attribute (default "on") when checked, and submits nothing when unchecked. For a binary boolean, read the checked property or the FormControl value in Angular.

<!-- Single boolean toggle -->
<label>
  <input type="checkbox" name="agree" value="yes" required />
  I agree to the terms
</label>

<!-- Multi-select with checkboxes -->
<fieldset>
  <legend>Interests</legend>
  <label><input type="checkbox" name="interest" value="angular" /> Angular</label>
  <label><input type="checkbox" name="interest" value="react"   /> React</label>
  <label><input type="checkbox" name="interest" value="vue"     /> Vue</label>
</fieldset>
In Angular reactive forms, a single FormControl bound to a checkbox holds true/false. For multi-select checkboxes, use a FormArray where each index maps to an option.
Radio buttons with the same name attribute form a group — selecting one deselects the others. Each has its own value, and the form submits the value of the checked radio.

<fieldset>
  <legend>Shipping speed</legend>
  <label><input type="radio" name="ship" value="standard"  /> Standard (5-7 days)</label>
  <label><input type="radio" name="ship" value="express"   /> Express (2 days)</label>
  <label><input type="radio" name="ship" value="overnight" /> Overnight</label>
</fieldset>

<!-- Angular template — bind the same formControlName to each radio -->
<input type="radio" value="standard"  formControlName="ship" />
<input type="radio" value="express"   formControlName="ship" />
<input type="radio" value="overnight" formControlName="ship" />

<!-- Single select (default) -->
<label for="country">Country</label>
<select id="country" name="country" required>
  <option value="">— choose —</option>
  <option value="us">United States</option>
  <option value="gb">United Kingdom</option>
  <option value="de">Germany</option>
</select>

<!-- Multi-select: hold Ctrl/Cmd to pick many -->
<select name="colors" multiple size="4">
  <option value="red">Red</option>
  <option value="green">Green</option>
  <option value="blue">Blue</option>
</select>
datalist provides autocomplete suggestions while still allowing free text. Unlike select, the user can type anything — the suggestions are not enforced as the only valid values.

<label for="framework">Framework</label>
<input id="framework" name="framework" list="framework-list"
       placeholder="Start typing…" />
<datalist id="framework-list">
  <option value="Angular">
  <option value="React">
  <option value="Vue">
  <option value="Svelte">
</datalist>
type="file" is the only input that cannot be controlled via formControlName because its value is read-only (a browser security restriction). Read selected files from the change event instead.

<!-- Single file upload -->
<input type="file" id="avatar" accept="image/png, image/jpeg" />

<!-- Multiple file upload -->
<input type="file" id="docs" multiple accept=".pdf,.docx" />

<!-- Capture from camera on mobile -->
<input type="file" accept="image/*" capture="environment" />
filters the OS file picker (e.g. image/*, .pdf, video/mp4) — it is not a security measure. Validate MIME type and size on the server.
allows selecting several files. Iterate over input.files as a FileList.
with value "user" (front camera) or "environment" (back camera) triggers the device camera directly on mobile.
To reset the file input programmatically, set input.value = '' directly on the DOM element — FormControl reset does not clear it.
Submit data invisible to the user
Cannot be validated; visible in DevTools
Default type for <button> if type not set
Reset fields to HTML initial values
Use for JS actions that don't submit

<!-- hidden: carry a CSRF token or record ID -->
<input type="hidden" name="_token" value="abc123" />
<input type="hidden" name="editId"  value="42" />

<!-- Always specify type on <button> to be explicit -->
<button type="submit">Save</button>
<button type="button" (click)="cancel()">Cancel</button>
<button type="reset">Clear form</button>
In Angular forms, avoid type="reset" because it resets to the HTML initial values, not Angular's FormGroup model. Call form.reset() for full control.
When a standard input does not fit your UI — a star rating, a tag chip input, a rich color swatch picker — implement ControlValueAccessor to make any component participate in Angular's form system.
is called when the parent model changes (setValue, patchValue, or initialization).
receives a callback — call it whenever the component's value changes.
receives a callback — call it when the user leaves the component.
is called when the control is disabled; update internal state to block interaction.
Credit card numbers, US ZIP codes, and phone numbers all contain leading zeros or separators. type="text" with inputmode="numeric" and a pattern is correct.
In Angular reactive forms the value is boolean true/false — do not compare to the string "true".
Forgetting to handle the file input reset.
Setting FormControl to null does not clear the file picker. Set input.value = '' on the DOM element.
Radio groups without a shared name.
Without identical name attributes, radio buttons do not form a mutual-exclusion group.
date/time values are strings not Date objects.
FormControl.value for date inputs is an ISO string. Parse with new Date() before comparison or arithmetic.
A <button> inside a <form> with no type defaults to type="submit", causing accidental form submissions.
Accessible forms are usable by people who navigate with keyboards, screen readers, or switch controls. These rules apply to every input type.
Billing address
Screen readers announce the label when the input receives focus. Without a label the field name is announced as the element type only ("edit text").
placeholder is NOT a label substitute — it disappears on input and has low contrast by default.
aria-describedby can point to a hint or error element to provide supplementary context without replacing the label.
Please enter a valid email address.
// Angular: set aria-invalid dynamically Please enter a valid email address.
Use the required attribute — this sets validity.valueMissing and also communicates the requirement to screen readers.
Visually mark required fields (e.g. an asterisk) and explain the convention once at the top of the form: "* Required fields".
Do not use color alone to indicate required status — users with color blindness cannot distinguish it.
The inputmode Attribute — Mobile Keyboard Hints

inputmode tells the browser which on-screen keyboard to display without changing the input's data type or validation. This is the correct tool for inputs where type semantics don't match keyboard needs.

<!-- 4-digit PIN: text type (no spinner), numeric keyboard -->
<input type="text" inputmode="numeric" pattern="\d{4}"
       maxlength="4" autocomplete="one-time-code"
       placeholder="0000" aria-label="PIN" />

<!-- Currency: decimal keyboard, text type to allow formatting -->
<input type="text" inputmode="decimal" name="amount"
       placeholder="0.00" pattern="\d+(\.\d{1,2})?" />

<!-- OTP: one-time password autofill -->
<input type="text" inputmode="numeric" autocomplete="one-time-code"
       pattern="\d{6}" maxlength="6" />
A realistic registration form exercises most input types and demonstrates how to combine validation, accessibility, and Angular reactive forms.
HTML offers 20+ input types — each tuned to a data kind, surfacing the right mobile keyboard and built-in validation.
Text-based types (text, email, password, url, tel, search) share attributes like pattern, maxlength, and autocomplete.
Numeric types (number, range) use min, max, and step; range needs an associated output element to display its value.
Date/time types always submit ISO 8601 strings; browser UI varies — consider a third-party picker for production.
Checkbox holds a boolean in reactive forms; radio buttons share a formControlName and each carries its own value.
Select supports single and multiple selection; datalist adds suggestions to a free-text input without restricting values.
file inputs are read-only — handle them via (change) events and validate size/MIME on both client and server.
Implement ControlValueAccessor to make any custom component a first-class citizen in Angular's form system.
